links.targetTitle: Cross-user sharing uses Swarm ACT with an ECDH-derived session key so only the publisher's Bee node can produce new grants.

global.title: Adapter storage uses AES-256-GCM with hierarchical feeds; sharing delegates to Swarm ACT
global.content:
Storage and recovery feature set:
- **AES-256-GCM encryption** at the app layer for operations and manifests.
- **Hierarchical feeds**: user → drive → document, letting the reader walk the tree lazily.
- **Folder topology preserved** across sync, recovery, and sharing.
- **Manifest compaction** — old op batches are merged so recovery is fast even after long history.

Sharing (ACT-protected):
- **Encrypted drive bundles** shared between users via Swarm IDs.
- **ECDH-based access control** through Swarm's native ACT (Access Control Trie). The Bee node handles decryption transparently, so the app layer does not perform any shared-secret math itself. This avoids a whole class of app-layer key-exchange bugs and delegates the crypto boundary to a well-audited primitive.
global.description: Storage encrypts ops and manifests with AES-256-GCM in hierarchical user/drive/document feeds; drive sharing delegates to Swarm's native ACT ECDH instead of app-layer shared secrets.
